item.text: I’ll review this as a design audit against the programme gates, feasibility evidence, literature, and repository workflow. I’ll first load the project’s bead instructions and identify the exact supporting documents, then return a ranked maintainer-facing verdict without changing files.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc 'bd prime && rg --files docs/next | sort && git status --short --branch'
/bin/bash -lc 'bd prime && rg --files docs/next | sort && git status --short --branch'
item.aggregated_output:
[bd prime] If this output is truncated by your host, read the full persisted hook output before continuing; it may contain project memories and session rules not visible in the preview.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

item.text: The worktree already contains substantial unrelated/uncommitted research output, including the design and literature directories. I’ll treat all of it as user-owned evidence and keep this strictly read-only. I’m now tracing ORACLE’s claims to the exact gates, boundary constraints, and resource ledger rather than judging it in isolation.

docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:53:> **VERDICT (verbatim): INCONCLUSIVE — PENDING (a) an NL-reaching parser [FK-NLB-3] and (b) the semantics core [g2 Π-soundness vs human gold, g3 necessity]. The instrument sub-claim is FEASIBLE-and-DEMONSTRATED but INSTRUMENT-ONLY; every MEASURED crossing from the instrument into real or natural input is, so far, NEGATIVE. SCOPE: the kernel-v0 / molecules-v0 instance, R0–1.7B hosts, the two tested verticals (family/world, code), the one pinned a1-hybrid deterministic front-end, the self-authored covered slice. CONFIDENCE: high on the instrument sub-claim (cross-vendor CONFIRMED); high that the current-build NL boundary and wild-formal boundary are negative at scope; the thesis-level verdict is INCONCLUSIVE because its two decisive legs (semantics core, a better parser) are unrun, not failed.**

docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:69:The measured boundary results, at full prominence and each scoped to its own envelope:
docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:71:- PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json, verdict FAIL] NL-boundary, family/world vertical: the a1-hybrid deterministic front-end retains only 251/527 = 47.6% of gold-parse covered exactness (Wilson UB 0.5121 vs the 0.90 bar) — a decisive FAIL. The mechanism is a STRUCTURAL paraphrase ceiling: the label-verbatim stratum parses 76.4% but the PARAPHRASE stratum parses 0/261 = 0.0% (single-label mapper, no synonym table). The failure is SAFE here — the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL did NOT fire (8/527 wrong, LB 0.0086 < 0.02); mis-parses become refusals.
docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:72:- PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/a5-nl.json, verdict FAIL, two independent causes] NL-boundary, code vertical: 356/855 = 41.6% (UB 0.4443) — FAIL — AND the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL FIRED (43/855 = 5.0% wrong, LB 0.0394 ≥ 0.02). This is the DANGEROUS failure mode the design named worse than any refusal: the frame-layer ROLE_DIR direction table flips containment orientation (contained-in 24, where-defined 18) into a real-but-wrong answer *with provenance attached*. Same front-end as l3a-parse, opposite safety outcome, because on the code vertical the frame direction table carries the semantics.
docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:74:- PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/g8.json, verdict FAIL, audit CONFIRMED] Wild-formal-content boundary: the profile-M fragment captures 0 of 1,000 random Mathlib declarations (UB 0.0027 vs the 1% gate) and the locator places 5/39 math concepts in Mathlib top-5 (12.8% vs the 80% gate), wrapped around a clean machinery PASS (F/B round-trip 33/33). Lean-minting is DEAD AT SCOPE (kernel size 39, signature-layer extraction) with a pre-registered bulk-authoring re-entry — not dead as a formalism claim.
docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:82:NL leg is now separately measured as a FAIL:
docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:84:PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/a5-llm.json verdict PASS, audit CONFIRMED per ledger] On the pinned 977-query kot-query-code/1 slice the deterministic engine scored conj 1.0 vs the best gate-valid LLM cell 0.3398 (+0.6602, one-sided LB +0.6346) at cost_ratio_min 22,836× and fabrication 78.7% on controls vs the engine's 0/122. DECISION: [MEASURED: registry/assessments/a5-llm.json does_not_license — the assessment's own scope ceiling, adopted verbatim] this is "a deterministic engine over its own extracted typed records beats a small closed-book/RAG LLM on a self-authored oracle-favourable code slice whose queries are already FORMAL" — there is no NL, no parse step, and NO conventional-substrate arm, so nothing distinguishes the kot-axiom kernel from ANY typed store + checker; the NL leg a5-llm deferred is exactly what a5-nl now measures = FAIL, so no code-vertical NL-usefulness claim survives the a5-llm PASS [MEASURED: a5-nl.json tree_impact].

docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:195:## 6. Bottom line
docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md:197:LOAD-BEARING: Neither thesis is dead; neither is established. Both stand at INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING within explicit scopes: correctness has a MEASURED, CONFIRMED *instrument* and a wall of MEASURED negatives at every boundary it has tried to cross, with its semantics core unrun; efficiency has one MEASURED, CONFIRMED end-task SIGN on formal inputs that is not yet attributable to kernel content, plus a large but upper-bound compression membership number, with its de-confound, cost, and consumption sides unrun [MEASURED: §1–§2 verdicts (registry/verdicts/*) restated; the cross-thesis reachability-wall-on-coverage-wall shape is separately an EXTRAPOLATION (ASM-0762), direction-only and never a premise]. The binding constraint on both verdicts is not compute — it is human annotation (f2b-transfer, g2 gold, human g9/M0a), one API key (A-F0), input-material generation (g3), and one cheap ablation (knull, after its control rewrite). Every decisive next step is already designed and pre-registered.

docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:918:| **P3-LR-RAG** — RAG, structured retrieval, knowledge graphs, tool-using neural baselines | [LIT] | P0 | Strongest current small-model RAG/tool-use recipes (the G4 comparator to beat); structured/KG retrieval vs dense retrieval; retrieval-index size/cost accounting practice; optimised/adaptive test-time compute (verify the ICLR-2025 result at source) + learned verifiers as baseline components; what published RAG wins say about "typed store + retrieval" vs "kernel semantics" attribution | P3-D-RAGC, P3-D-FRONTIER, P3-D-GNN (co-input) |
docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:919:| **P3-LR-PARSE** — semantic parsing, program synthesis, grammar induction, uncertainty + selective prediction | [LIT] | P0 | State of semantic parsing into executable forms at small model scale; NL→DSL program synthesis with execution feedback; grammar induction for closed grammars; calibrated abstention/selective prediction (risk-coverage curves, conformal methods) for parse confidence; why l3a-parse/a5-nl-CLASS failures happened in the literature and what fixed them (capability-limited vs fundamental per failure); paraphrase robustness methods | P3-D-NLB, P3-D-PS, P3-D-ORACLE (co) |
docs/next/programme-3-neurosymbolic-architecture.md:923:| **P3-LR-SURG** — model compression, dimension/structured pruning, distillation + the EXACT invariances of transformer model surgery | [LIT] | P1 | Structured pruning + retrained-recovery + KD best practice (the (iv) comparator); rotation/absorb methods and their PROVEN invariance conditions per architecture component (norms, residual, heads, tied embeddings) — causal representation analysis; SAE/dictionary-feature geometry (overcompleteness, non-orthogonality) and interp-guided pruning attempts; published size-vs-capability curves 100M–2B; whether ANY prior work dropped semantically-identified parameters and backfilled from an external store (H-DD's closest prior art) | P3-D-DD |

docs/next/lit/SYS.md:656:## 12. Source count and verification ledger

221	- **Repair attribution:** Δ_repair(j) = M(REPAIR(j)) − M(FULL-REAL) — the
   222	  single-stage recovery.
   223	- **Interaction report (mandatory):** I(j) = Δ_prefix(j) − Δ_repair(j), reported per
   224	  stage with its paired CI. Interactions are EXPECTED (an upstream fix can unmask a
   225	  downstream failure); the protocol does not force additivity — it measures the
   226	  non-additivity and gates the claim language on it (§8, IG-3) [STIPULATED: proposed
   227	  ASM-ORC-2; the honesty mechanism is reporting, not assumption].
   228	
   229	**Claim-language rule:** "stage j is responsible for X of the loss" may be published
   230	only when |I(j)| is below the pre-registered interaction bound θ_int; otherwise the
   231	report publishes the prefix chain and repair vector side-by-side and the attribution
   232	is stated as JOINT over the interacting stage set — never silently resolved
   233	[STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2].
   234	
   235	### 4.3 Ordering
   236	
   237	The prefix ladder runs in pipeline order (upstream first) — the natural
   238	"conditioned on gold upstream" reading. Pipeline order is itself a choice that can
   239	hide order-dependence; the repair vector is the built-in cross-check (a repair
   240	splice is order-free), and the optional 2^K factorial removes the dependence
   241	entirely where cheap [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2].
   242	
   243	### 4.4 The terminal residual (USE)
   244	
   245	After all spliceable stages are oracled (FULL-ORACLE), any remaining shortfall from
   246	the family's G2 ceiling is attributed to the terminal consumer: USE for fusion arms
   247	(the model does not integrate delivered signal — the nsk1 shape), or
   248	EXECUTE+RENDER integration for engine arms. Formally: L_USE = M_ceiling −
   249	M(FULL-ORACLE), where M_ceiling is the family's pre-registered G2 ceiling (for
   250	engine families: the measured covered-exact bound [MEASURED: l3a/a5]; for fusion
   251	families: the family prereg's stated ceiling). A large L_USE with a PASSING delivery
   252	probe is the measured nsk1 signature — delivery without integration — and the
   253	protocol makes it a named, reportable outcome instead of a diffuse miss
   254	[STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-1/2; motivated by MEASURED nsk1].
   255	
   256	### 4.5 The observational layer (per-item stage trace)
   257	
   258	Every run (all configs) logs a per-item trace: each stage's input/output artifact
   259	content-hash, typed outcome code (OK / ERR_* fail-closed / ABSTAIN / DIVERGED-FROM-
   260	GOLD-AT-<stage> where gold exists), per-stage confidence score where the stage emits
   261	one, and µs/token cost. The trace supplies (a) the first-divergence-point label per
   262	failed item — the generalisation of l3a-parse's frame-miss/gazetteer-miss counters —
   263	and (b) the per-stage risk-coverage curves (§7.3). **Authority rule:** where trace
   264	and splice disagree, the splice (interventional) attribution is authoritative; the
   265	trace is diagnosis granularity [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2 — counters label
   266	visible failure, splices establish causal responsibility].
   267	
   268	---
   269	
   270	## 5. Oracle artifacts: provenance, authoring, and the no-smuggling rules
   271	
   272	[STIPULATED throughout: proposed ASM-ORC-3.]
   273	
   274	1. **Pinned before run.** Every oracle corpus (gold parses, gold record maps, gold
   275	   subgraphs, gold engine results, canonical surface forms) is content-hashed and
   276	   pinned in the family's prereg BEFORE any splice run; growing or editing an oracle
   277	   corpus after first use is a new corpus version and restarts that family's ladder.
   278	2. **Blind authoring.** Gold artifacts are authored blind to any system output —
   279	   from the item + store only. Where the two measured verticals already have gold
   280	   (l3a/a5 gold parses; f2b's d-qa-r record↔item map), those pins are REUSED, not
   281	   re-authored [MEASURED: those corpora exist as frozen pins; reuse is a cost fact].
   282	3. **Gold quality is measured, not assumed.** New gold corpora require dual
   283	   independent authoring on a pre-registered sample (≥10% or 100 items, whichever is
   284	   larger) with agreement ≥ the pre-registered bar (default 0.95 artifact-exactness;
   285	   family preregs may tighten); disagreements adjudicated and logged. A corpus
   286	   failing the bar is not an oracle [STIPULATED: fail-closed gold gate, IG-4 §8].
   287	4. **Answerability pre-tagging.** Every eval item carries an independent tag —
   288	   `answerable-covered | answerable-uncovered | ambiguous | unanswerable` — assigned
   289	   BEFORE any scoring, by the gold-authoring pass. For `ambiguous`/`unanswerable`
   290	   items the gold PARSE artifact is ABSTAIN (abstention is the correct output), and
   291	   for `answerable-uncovered` the gold EXECUTE artifact is the fail-closed refusal —
   292	   so abstentions are creditable and a stage is never penalised for correctly
   293	   refusing [STIPULATED: adopted from PARSE.md §4/§5d (amp-2024, dte-2023,
   294	   triagesql-2020 lineage); this is the abstain-credit rule].
   295	5. **Coverage disclosure.** Items lacking a defined gold artifact at stage j are
   296	   EXCLUDED from stage-j splice cells; the exclusion count and reason are published
   297	   per cell (no silent truncation — the programme's no-silent-caps norm). An
   298	   exclusion rate > 20% in any cell voids that cell's attribution claim
   299	   [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-3].
   300	6. **No oracle leakage into real arms.** The real-regime stages must be bit-isolated
   301	   from oracle corpora at run time (separate process inputs; the harness passes a
   302	   config-scoped artifact bundle) — a real arm that could read gold is a broken run,
   303	   ERR_ORACLE_LEAK, fail-closed [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-3].
   304	7. **Labelling at the artifact level.** Every result row, plot, and report table
   305	   carries `input_regime` (`full-real`, `prefix:<stage>`, `repair:<stage>`,
   306	   `full-oracle`); any figure quoted anywhere outside the report must carry the
   307	   label. Full-real is always the FIRST column [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-3; the
   308	   anti-smuggling surface].
   309	
   310	---
   311	
   312	## 6. Instrumentation specification
   313	
   314	[STIPULATED throughout: proposed ASM-ORC-4 — implementation choices.]
   315	
   316	- **Home:** `poc/oracle/` (harness code stays out of `encoder/`, repo convention).
   317	  Zero new runtime deps; node/python per existing harness practice; `nice -n 10`,
   318	  checkpointed per box discipline.
   319	- **Stage interface contract:** every participating architecture implements
   320	  `run_stage(stage_id, item_id, upstream_artifact, config) → artifact | ERR_*`, and
   321	  the harness owns the splice: at an oracled stage it substitutes the pinned gold
   322	  artifact and never calls the real stage. Architectures that cannot expose a stage
   323	  boundary cannot claim that boundary's attribution — the limitation is published in
   324	  their prereg (expected for tightly-fused arms at ENCODE, where the probe
   325	  substitutes, §3.1).
   326	- **Config schema** (JSON, hash-pinned per run):
   327	  `{family, vertical, stage_map: [...], regimes: {stage: real|oracle},
   328	  oracle_corpus_hashes: {...}, endpoint_id, seed, n_items, item_corpus_hash}`.
   329	- **Trace record** (JSONL, one per item × config):
   330	  `{item_id, config_hash, tags: {answerability}, perturbation_class,
   331	  stages: [{stage, regime, in_hash, out_hash, outcome: OK|ABSTAIN|ERR_*,
   332	  diverged_from_gold: bool|null, confidence: float|null, cost_us|tokens}],
   333	  endpoint: {answered: bool, correct: bool|null, dangerous_wrong: bool},
   334	  }` — dangerous_wrong = answered ∧ wrong ∧ provenance-attached, the a5-nl S2 event
   335	  definition [MEASURED: a5-nl S2 class, adopted as the event type].
   336	- **Determinism:** deterministic stages byte-reproducible; LM stages seed-pinned
   337	  (greedy or pinned-seed sampling per family prereg); every run twice-executed on a
   338	  1% item sample as a reproducibility smoke check (IG-2).
   339	- **Error codes:** ERR_ORACLE_LEAK, ERR_GOLD_MISSING, ERR_STAGE_CONTRACT,
   340	  ERR_CONFIG_HASH — all fail-closed, run aborts, no partial report (house style: no
   341	  silent fallbacks).
   342	- **Perturbation typing:** the NL item corpus carries a perturbation-class tag per
   343	  phrasing (minimum taxonomy: `label-verbatim | synonym | structural-paraphrase |
   344	  noise(typo/ASR)`; extendable per vertical), enabling the perturbation × stage
   345	  matrix (§7.5) — the Dr.Spider-shaped practice, K>1 phrasings per query from a
   346	  source disjoint from any calibration set [STIPULATED: adopted from PARSE.md §6.5;
   347	  the measured l3a-parse/a5-nl used K=1, below field practice — this protocol
   348	  requires K≥3 phrasings per item for new corpora, K=1 legacy corpora usable only
   349	  for the shakedown].
   350	
   351	---
   352	
   353	## 7. Metrics and statistical specification
   354	
   355	### 7.1 Endpoint discipline
   356	
   357	Each family × vertical decomposition names ONE pre-registered endpoint scalar M
   358	(inherited from the family's G2/G3 prereg — e.g. covered-exactness for engine
   359	families, item accuracy for fusion families) and ALWAYS co-reports the selective
   360	triple: coverage (fraction answered), selective risk P(wrong | answered), and the
   361	dangerous-wrong rate P(answered ∧ wrong ∧ provenance) — because abstention is part
   362	of every product and the two risk notions diverge exactly when coverage drops
   363	[STIPULATED: adopted from PARSE.md §8 gate-instrument requirement (1)].
   364	
   365	### 7.2 The survival fraction σ (the K-P3v2(5) quantity)
   366	
   367	For family F with mechanism system S and its pre-registered no-mechanism control C
   368	(the family's factorial control cell, programme §2.2):
   369	
   370	- γ_G2 = M(S, G2-ANCHOR) − M(C, G2-ANCHOR)
   371	- γ_G3 = M(S, FULL-REAL) − M(C, FULL-REAL)
   372	- **σ = γ_G3 / γ_G2**, defined only when the one-sided 95% LCB of γ_G2 > 0
   373	  (otherwise σ is N/A and the family has no G2 effect to lose — G2 already failed).
   374	
   375	σ with its one-sided 95% CI (hierarchical bootstrap, paired items, per house rules
   376	§2.5) is THE number K-P3v2(5) consumes: "G2 gain fails to survive G3 at the
   377	pre-registered retention margin" ≡ UCB95(σ) < σ_min for the family, with σ_min set
   378	in the family's prereg, not here [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-5; the margin is the
   379	family's choice, the estimator is this protocol's]. Note both S and C cross the NL
   380	boundary (2×2 minimum: {S,C} × {G2-ANCHOR, FULL-REAL}); the splice ladder refines
   381	the S column; running the ladder on C too is OPTIONAL (recommended when γ decay is
   382	ambiguous between "S degrades" and "C improves") [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2].
   383	
   384	### 7.3 Per-stage risk-coverage
   385	
   386	For every stage that emits a confidence score, the report includes the stage's
   387	risk-coverage curve (risk vs coverage as the stage's abstention threshold sweeps)
   388	and AUACC, computed at FULL-REAL and at the stage's repair splice — a scalar
   389	operating point is never the only view [STIPULATED: PARSE.md §8, adopted verbatim].
   390	
   391	### 7.4 Stage-artifact metrics
   392	
   393	As tabled in §3.1 — each stage has an artifact-level match function against gold
   394	(whole-frame exactness for PARSE, never intent-accuracy/slot-F1 alone [STIPULATED:
   395	PARSE.md §2 metric discipline, adopted]; set P/R@k for RETRIEVE; graph F1 for
   396	CONSTRUCT; probe keyacc for ENCODE; exactness for EXECUTE; result-preservation for
   397	RENDER). Artifact metrics are DIAGNOSTIC covariates; endpoint splices are the
   398	attribution [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2 authority rule].
   399	
   400	### 7.5 The two attribution matrices
   401	
   402	1. **Stage-loss vector:** {Δ_prefix(j)}, {Δ_repair(j)}, {I(j)} with paired 95% BCa
   403	   bootstrap CIs (items paired across configs — identical item sets by
   404	   construction), plus the USE residual (§4.4).
   405	2. **Perturbation × stage matrix:** Δ_prefix(j) recomputed per perturbation class —
   406	   which phrasing class breaks which stage (worst-class reported, not aggregate
   407	   only) [STIPULATED: Dr.Spider-shaped practice per PARSE.md §6.5, adopted].
   408	
   409	### 7.6 S2 (dangerous-wrong) attribution rule — mechanical
   410	
   411	For every dangerous-wrong event at FULL-REAL: the event is attributed to stage j iff
   412	REPAIR(j) flips that item to correct-or-abstain; if multiple stages flip it, it is
   413	multi-attributed (reported in a separate joint bucket, never split fractionally); if
   414	NO single repair flips it, it is attributed JOINT-UPSTREAM (the smallest prefix that
   415	flips it) [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2]. Per-stage S2 counts carry Wilson bounds;
   416	the known-answer test is a5-nl's 43 measured events, whose mechanism is a PARSE.c
   417	table defect — the shakedown must recover that attribution (§8) [MEASURED: a5-nl
   418	S2 = 43/855, mechanism per its assessment; the recovery requirement is the test].
   419	Standing caution carried on every S2 line: confidence scores can FAIL to separate
   420	systematic high-confidence inversions (the conformal-abstention caveat), so S2
   421	attribution never relies on stage confidence — only on splice flips [STIPULATED:
   422	PARSE.md §4/§5c, adopted].
   423	
   424	### 7.7 Sample sizes and power
   425	
   426	Default n per vertical: the full pinned item corpus (the two existing verticals:
   427	527 and 855 items — sizes at which the measured Wilson intervals were decision-grade
   428	[MEASURED: l3a-parse/a5-nl envelopes]). For new corpora: pre-register n such that a
   429	stage loss of Δ = 0.05 (endpoint scale) is detectable at 80% power under paired
   430	McNemar-style comparison — n ≈ 500–1000 covers this for endpoint variances in the
   431	measured range; the family prereg states its exact computation [STIPULATED: proposed
   432	ASM-ORC-5; the power target 0.05/80% is the protocol default, family-overridable
   433	upward only]. Multiplicity: the stage-loss vector is DIAGNOSTIC (no verdict fires
   434	from it); σ is the only gate-feeding estimand and gets the one-sided CI; no FWER
   435	correction is applied to diagnostic vectors, and this is stated on every report
   436	[STIPULATED: house §2.5 rule 5 — one primary per experiment — applied].
   437	
   438	---
   439	
   440	## 8. Instrument gates and the protocol's own falsifiers
   441	
   442	The protocol is itself an instrument and must be validated on known answers before
   443	it judges any new architecture [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-6; programme §2.4
   444	calibration norm, applied to this instrument].
   445	
   446	**Instrument gates (every family run, fail-closed):**
   447	
   448	- **IG-1 (ceiling reproduction):** the G2-ANCHOR config must reproduce the family's
   449	  registered G2 result within its pre-registered tolerance (deterministic verticals:
   450	  exactly; LM endpoints: within the seed-pinned reproduction band). Failure = harness
   451	  bug; no attribution is published.
   452	- **IG-2 (determinism):** the 1% twice-run sample must match (byte-exact for
   453	  deterministic stages; endpoint-exact for seed-pinned LM stages).
   454	- **IG-3 (interaction bound):** if max_j |I(j)| > θ_int (default 0.10 endpoint
   455	  scale, family-overridable downward only), single-stage claim language is BARRED
   456	  for the interacting stages (§4.2) — the report still publishes, with joint
   457	  attribution only.
   458	- **IG-4 (gold quality):** dual-authoring agreement ≥ bar (§5.3) on every new oracle
   459	  corpus.
   460	- **IG-5 (exclusion ceiling):** per-cell exclusions ≤ 20% (§5.5).

    21	---
    22	
    23	## 0. One-paragraph summary
    24	
    25	Every Programme-3 architecture family must pass through gate **G2** (does the
    26	mechanism help at all, on oracle inputs?) and gate **G3** (how much of that survives
    27	real natural-language input?) before any frontier attempt [STIPULATED:
    28	ASM-0817, restated]. The measured history says the G2→G3 crossing is where this
    29	programme dies or lives: both measured NL crossings FAILED (l3a-parse 47.6%
    30	retention, a5-nl 41.6% + the S2 dangerous-wrong kill) while the engine beneath them
    31	was exact (600/600, 855/855) [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/{l3a-parse,a5-nl,l3a,a5}.json,
    32	restated within their envelopes per feasibility-synthesis §1]. This document
    33	specifies **KOT-DECOMP/1**: a per-family, per-vertical protocol that runs each
    34	architecture under a ladder of *oracle-splice configurations* — from all-real to
    35	all-oracle, one stage boundary at a time — plus per-item stage tracing, so that any
    36	G2→G3 performance drop is **attributed to a named stage** (NL-parse and its four
    37	sub-stages / retrieval-addressing / graph-construction / graph-encoding-delivery /
    38	execution / rendering / terminal LM-use residual) with paired confidence intervals,
    39	per-stage risk-coverage reporting, and a mechanical S2 (dangerous-wrong) attribution
    40	rule — instead of remaining an ambiguous end-to-end delta. The protocol is a
    41	**diagnostic instrument**: every configuration containing ANY oracled stage is
    42	`oracle-diagnostic` under ASM-0814 and licenses NO W1 claim and no real-input
    43	usefulness claim of any kind [STIPULATED: ASM-0814, applied]. The complete protocol
    44	is a design EXTRAPOLATION assembled from field-attested ingredients (gold-parse
    45	arms, stage counters, perturbation-typed attribution, ambiguity pre-tagging) — it is
    46	therefore validated on a **known-answer shakedown** (P3-E-ORACLE-0, §8) against the
    47	two already-measured verticals before any family uses it [EXTRAPOLATION → tested:
    48	docs/next/lit/PARSE.md §8 P3-D-ORACLE scope, adopted; the shakedown is the test].
    49	
    50	---
    51	
    52	## 1. Where this protocol sits (and what it must serve)
    53	
    54	1. **The G3 gate instrument.** Programme rev-2 §4 defines G3 as "how much of the G2
    55	   effect survives realistic NL mapping", with this design named as the instrument
    56	   that "attributes the G2→G3 loss (parse vs retrieval vs execution vs generation)"
    57	   [STIPULATED: ASM-0817, adopted as this document's requirement].
    58	2. **The K-P3v2(5) kill feed.** Programme §7.1 kills the competitiveness programme if
    59	   "every family's G2 gain fails to survive G3 at the pre-registered retention
    60	   margin" — this protocol defines the survival quantity σ (§7.2) mechanically, so
    61	   that kill condition is computable, not narrative [STIPULATED: ASM-0818, served].
    62	3. **The four-stage H-GNN decomposition.** Programme §3.2 mandates "retrieval → graph
    63	   construction → graph encoding → LM use, separately instrumented"; KOT-DECOMP/1 is
    64	   that instrumentation, generalised to all families [STIPULATED: ASM-0815/0805 scope,
    65	   served].
    66	4. **The NL-boundary wall, respected structurally.** Every natural-input
    67	   store-addressed leg is NLB-gated or oracle-labelled [STIPULATED: ASM-0814].
    68	   KOT-DECOMP/1 runs BOTH regimes by construction; §9 states exactly which runs are
    69	   permitted pre-NLB-clearance and what nothing here licenses.
    70	5. **KOT-FAIR/2 slot.** Decomposition reports are a mandatory co-publication of every
    71	   G4 attempt (the attribution annex), and the protocol's runs are exempt from the
    72	   P3-E-CAL calibration gate because they make no index claim — the same exemption
    73	   class as P3-E-NLB-1 and the G1 censuses [STIPULATED: programme §4 sequencing
    74	   exemption, extended to this instrument; proposed ASM-ORC-5 §11].
    75	
    76	What it must NOT do: manufacture a W1-relevant number from an oracle leg (the
    77	anti-l3a/a5 rule — the parser is part of the product) [STIPULATED: ASM-0808(NL-input
    78	integrity), restated]; and never let a single "gold-parse arm" stand in for stage
    79	attribution — correlated upstream errors make one splice point uninterpretable
    80	[STIPULATED: adopted verbatim from docs/next/lit/PARSE.md §8 P3-D-ORACLE: "a single
    81	gold-parse arm cannot localize correlated errors adequately"].
    82	
    83	---
    84	
    85	## 2. The measured anchor: what already exists and why it is not enough
    86	
    87	What exists (the protocol's inheritance, all already field-shaped):
    88	
    89	- **A G2 anchor per vertical.** The l3a/a5 verdicts ARE the gold-parse arm: engine
    90	  covered-exactness 600/600 and 855/855 under gold formal inputs, µs cost
    91	  [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/{l3a,a5}.json, audit CONFIRMED, instrument-only scope].
    92	- **Per-stage failure counters.** l3a-parse already logs a stage breakdown
    93	  (frame-miss 228 / mapper-abstain 48 / gazetteer-miss 40 / frame-ambiguous 41) as a
    94	  secondary endpoint (`/analysis/parse_stage_breakdown`) [MEASURED:
    95	  registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json endpoint sec-stage-breakdown].
    96	- **A measured dangerous-class mechanism.** a5-nl's S2 kill traced to a deterministic
    97	  ROLE_DIR direction-table defect — a *sub-stage* of parsing (role/direction
    98	  binding), not "parsing" generically [MEASURED: registry/assessments/a5-nl.json, as
    99	  restated in PARSE.md §1].
   100	- **A measured delivery-vs-use separation.** nsk1 showed content can be DELIVERED at
   101	  echo grade while INTEGRATION into behaviour fails (R− rescue 0/8) — so
   102	  encode-delivery and LM-use must be separately measurable [MEASURED:
   103	  registry/assessments/nsk1-{bprime2,g2d}.json, exploratory/DRAFT status, restated
   104	  per feasibility-synthesis §1].
   105	
   106	Why that is not enough, per the lit-review this design is blocked by [STIPULATED:
   107	adopted from PARSE.md §8]:
   108	
   109	1. Observational counters label the *first visible* failure, not the *causally
   110	   responsible* stage: a parse that is "close enough" by the artifact metric can
   111	   still poison retrieval addressing downstream; counters alone cannot see that.
   112	2. One gold-parse splice point cannot separate parse loss from retrieval loss from
   113	   rendering loss when several stages cross the NL boundary at once (dense retrieval
   114	   over NL queries, NL-conditioned rendering).
   115	3. Abstentions must be creditable: without pre-scoring ambiguity/answerability tags,
   116	   a correct abstention on an ambiguous item is indistinguishable from a stage
   117	   failure (should-abstain detection is itself measured-hard: TriageSQL ~60% F1)
   118	   [STIPULATED: PARSE.md §4/§5d, adopted].
   119	4. Scalars hide the operating point: each stage must report its risk-coverage curve,
   120	   not one number [STIPULATED: PARSE.md §8, adopted verbatim].

   123	
   124	## 3. The canonical stage taxonomy and per-family instantiation
   125	
   126	### 3.1 Canonical stages (the superset; every family instantiates a subset, in order)
   127	
   128	[STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-1 — the taxonomy is a design choice; stage names are
   129	deliberately NOT "S1/S2" to avoid collision with the S1/S2 kill-criteria namespace.]
   130	
   131	| Stage id | Name | Input → output artifact | Oracle artifact (§5) | Stage-artifact metric (§7.4) |
   132	|---|---|---|---|---|
   133	| **PARSE** | NL → formal query/claim | NL string → typed formal object (kot-query/1 or family DSL) | gold logical form | whole-frame exactness (never slot-F1 alone) |
   134	| — PARSE.a | intent/frame selection | NL → frame/family id | gold frame id | frame accuracy |
   135	| — PARSE.b | entity/schema linking | NL + frame → bound entities/URNs | gold entity bindings | binding exactness |
   136	| — PARSE.c | role/direction binding | frame + entities → directed roles | gold direction bits | both-orientation accuracy, per directional frame |
   137	| — PARSE.d | serialization | bound frame → DSL string/AST | gold serialization | AST equality |
   138	| **RETRIEVE** | store addressing | formal query (or NL, if the arm retrieves from NL) → record/rule/node id set | gold item→record map | set P/R@k vs gold; address exactness |
   139	| **CONSTRUCT** | graph construction | record/node set → typed subgraph | gold subgraph | graph F1 (nodes, edges, labels) |
   140	| **ENCODE** | encoding + delivery | subgraph/records → model-consumable signal (GNN embedding, soft tokens, KV pairs, continuation-set mask) | gold-input-encoding (upstream-oracle) + delivery probe | delivery probe keyacc (nsk1 instrument shape) |
   141	| **EXECUTE** | deterministic engine | formal query + store → checked result + provenance | gold engine result | exactness (engine is exact where covered — expected ≈ oracle) |
   142	| **RENDER** | surface generation from checked result | checked result → NL answer | canonical surface form (verbatim record answer / pinned template) | result-preservation match (the g2d corruption check) |
   143	| **USE** | terminal LM integration (residual, not spliceable) | all delivered signal → behaviour | none — defined as the residual under all-upstream-oracle (§4.4) | endpoint residual |
   144	
   145	Notes binding the taxonomy:
   146	
   147	- PARSE sub-stages a–d are mandatory wherever a parser exists — this granularity is
   148	  what makes the measured a5-nl mechanism (a PARSE.c defect) expressible
   149	  [STIPULATED: PARSE.md §8 "intent/frame selection, role/direction binding, entity
   150	  linking, DSL serialization, execution, rendering", adopted verbatim].
   151	- ENCODE is **probe-instrumented, splice-limited**: a "gold encoding" of a learned
   152	  encoder is not well-defined, so ENCODE's upstream splice is "encoder applied to the
   153	  gold-constructed input", and the encode-vs-use separation comes from the delivery
   154	  probe (can the signal be read back?) vs the endpoint (does behaviour improve?) —
   155	  the nsk1 lesson turned into instrumentation [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-1;
   156	  motivated by MEASURED nsk1 delivery/integration split].
   157	- EXECUTE is expected to contribute ≈0 loss on the covered slice (the engine is
   158	  exact and fail-closed there [MEASURED: l3a/a5]); it stays in the ladder anyway
   159	  because integration bugs at the engine seam are exactly what a splice catches
   160	  cheaply [STIPULATED: design choice, ~zero marginal cost — the engine is µs-class].
   161	
   162	### 3.2 Per-family instantiation (the boundary set B(F))
   163	
   164	For each family F, define **B(F) = the ordered set of stages whose input regime
   165	differs between the G2 (oracle-input) and G3 (natural-input) runs**. The
   166	decomposition runs over B(F); stages outside B(F) are identical in both regimes and
   167	are attributed zero by construction [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-1].
   168	
   169	| Family | Instantiated pipeline | B(F) (typical) | Family-specific notes |
   170	|---|---|---|---|
   171	| **H-VL** (verifier loop) | PARSE → RETRIEVE → EXECUTE → USE(retry/accept policy) → RENDER | PARSE(a–d), RETRIEVE | the retry policy is part of USE; f2b's record→item alignment is the RETRIEVE gold map |
   172	| **H-PS** (NL→program synthesis) | PARSE(a–d) → EXECUTE → RENDER | PARSE(a–d), RENDER | RENDER is first-class here: generation-from-checked-result is nsk1-g2d-cautioned [MEASURED: g2d net-harmful text append, exploratory] |
   173	| **H-GNN-ST/KV/LF** | PARSE(query understanding) → RETRIEVE → CONSTRUCT → ENCODE → USE | PARSE, RETRIEVE, CONSTRUCT (+ENCODE probe) | this IS the programme §3.2 four-stage decomposition; oracle-subgraph vs NL-extracted-subgraph are the two named control cells |
   174	| **H-RULE-CD** | PARSE(span/coverage detection) → RETRIEVE(rule selection) → EXECUTE(continuation-set derivation) → ENCODE(mask delivery) → USE | PARSE, RETRIEVE | CD is transport: the engine derives, masking delivers [STIPULATED: ASM-0815, restated] |
   175	| **H-RULE-KV** | RETRIEVE(rule/record selection from input) → ENCODE(KV construction) → USE | RETRIEVE (+PARSE if NL-keyed) | provenance claims need the splice/ablation evidence, never attention maps [STIPULATED: ASM-0815] |
   176	| **H-DD** (store reinjection legs) | RETRIEVE(store addressing) → ENCODE(reinjection) → USE | RETRIEVE | the oracle-addressed reinjection leg of P3-E-DD-0 step (4) is exactly a RETRIEVE-oracle splice [STIPULATED: ASM-0816 alignment] |
   177	
   178	Each family's Phase-2 prereg MUST contain a completed instantiation row: its stage
   179	map, its B(F), its oracle corpora (hash-pinned), and its endpoint — §10 provides the
   180	prereg checklist block [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-5].
   181	
   182	---
   183	
   184	## 4. The splice-ladder protocol (the interventional core)
   185	
   186	### 4.1 Configurations
   187	
   188	A configuration c is an assignment `regime(stage) ∈ {real, oracle}` for every stage
   189	in B(F). The MANDATORY set per family × vertical [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2]:
   190	
   191	1. **FULL-REAL** — every stage real. The only configuration whose numbers may ever
   192	   feed a G3/G4 reading; still diagnostic-labelled under this protocol (§9).
   193	2. **PREFIX ladder** — for j = 1…K (K = |B(F)|, pipeline order): stages 1…j oracle,
   194	   the rest real. PREFIX(K) = FULL-ORACLE. This yields the telescoped loss chain.
   195	3. **REPAIR splices** — for each j: stage j alone oracle, all others real. This
   196	   answers "how much does fixing stage j alone buy?" — the deployment-relevant
   197	   question (which stage should P3-D-NLB-class investment target first).
   198	4. **G2-ANCHOR** — the family's registered G2 configuration (oracle through the
   199	   PARSE boundary, everything downstream real). For H-VL/H-PS this coincides with
   200	   PREFIX(PARSE.d); it is named separately because it must reproduce the family's
   201	   registered G2 result (instrument gate IG-1, §8).
   202	
   203	Config count: K+1 prefix + K repair + full-real ≤ 2K+2 ≤ 14 at K ≤ 6 — small enough
   204	that the full ladder fits tiny-scale budgets (§10) [STIPULATED: sizing choice].
   205	OPTIONAL at R-1 where the per-config cost is trivial (deterministic verticals,
   206	CPU-only): the full 2^K factorial, which upgrades the interaction report (§4.3) from
   207	a bound to an exact Shapley decomposition — never required [STIPULATED: proposed
   208	ASM-ORC-2; Shapley over 2^K configs is standard cooperative-game attribution, cited
   209	as method shape only, no empirical claim].
   210	
   211	### 4.2 Attribution semantics
   212	
   213	Let M(c) be the family's pre-registered endpoint under configuration c, always
   214	reported as the selective triple (coverage, selective risk, dangerous-wrong rate)
   215	plus the scalar the family prereg names (§7.1).
   216	
   217	- **Prefix (telescoped) attribution:** Δ_prefix(j) = M(PREFIX(j)) − M(PREFIX(j−1)),
   218	  with PREFIX(0) = FULL-REAL. By construction Σ_j Δ_prefix(j) = M(FULL-ORACLE) −
   219	  M(FULL-REAL) exactly — the total G2→G3-shaped loss, telescoped with no residual
   220	  [STIPULATED: arithmetic identity of the ladder, proposed ASM-ORC-2].

461	
   462	**P3-E-ORACLE-0 — the known-answer shakedown (the design's falsifier; new bead §12):**
   463	rebuild the ladder on the two ALREADY-MEASURED verticals (a1-hybrid front-end +
   464	engine, frozen pins reused; CPU-only, ~$0) and require, pre-registered:
   465	
   466	1. **KA-1:** FULL-ORACLE reproduces l3a/a5 covered-exactness exactly (600/600,
   467	   855/855 slice behaviour) [MEASURED anchor: those verdicts].
   468	2. **KA-2:** the decomposition attributes ≥ 95% of the measured G2→G3 loss to the
   469	   PARSE stage family on both verticals — the known truth, because in the measured
   470	   system the parser is the ONLY stage crossing the boundary (everything downstream
   471	   is the exact deterministic engine) [MEASURED: l3a-parse/a5-nl mechanism; the bar
   472	   is the falsifier — if the ladder puts material loss anywhere else on this system,
   473	   the protocol is broken].
   474	3. **KA-3:** sub-stage attribution reproduces the measured stage breakdown shape on
   475	   l3a-parse (frame-miss-dominant; paraphrase stratum failing at PARSE.a/PARSE.b)
   476	   within pre-registered tolerance [MEASURED: parse_stage_breakdown 228/48/40/41].
   477	4. **KA-4:** the S2 rule (§7.6) attributes ≥ 90% of a5-nl's 43 dangerous-wrong
   478	   events to PARSE.c (the ROLE_DIR direction table), and the PARSE.c repair splice
   479	   drives measured dangerous-wrong LB below the 0.02 bar on that vertical

243	### 4.4 The terminal residual (USE)
   244	
   245	After all spliceable stages are oracled (FULL-ORACLE), any remaining shortfall from
   246	the family's G2 ceiling is attributed to the terminal consumer: USE for fusion arms
   247	(the model does not integrate delivered signal — the nsk1 shape), or
   248	EXECUTE+RENDER integration for engine arms. Formally: L_USE = M_ceiling −
   249	M(FULL-ORACLE), where M_ceiling is the family's pre-registered G2 ceiling (for
   250	engine families: the measured covered-exact bound [MEASURED: l3a/a5]; for fusion
   251	families: the family prereg's stated ceiling). A large L_USE with a PASSING delivery
   252	probe is the measured nsk1 signature — delivery without integration — and the
   253	protocol makes it a named, reportable outcome instead of a diffuse miss
   254	[STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-1/2; motivated by MEASURED nsk1].
   255	
   256	### 4.5 The observational layer (per-item stage trace)
   257	
   258	Every run (all configs) logs a per-item trace: each stage's input/output artifact
   259	content-hash, typed outcome code (OK / ERR_* fail-closed / ABSTAIN / DIVERGED-FROM-
   260	GOLD-AT-<stage> where gold exists), per-stage confidence score where the stage emits
   261	one, and µs/token cost. The trace supplies (a) the first-divergence-point label per
   262	failed item — the generalisation of l3a-parse's frame-miss/gazetteer-miss counters —
   263	and (b) the per-stage risk-coverage curves (§7.3). **Authority rule:** where trace
   264	and splice disagree, the splice (interventional) attribution is authoritative; the
   265	trace is diagnosis granularity [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-2 — counters label
   266	visible failure, splices establish causal responsibility].
   267	
   268	---
   269	
   270	## 5. Oracle artifacts: provenance, authoring, and the no-smuggling rules
   271	
   272	[STIPULATED throughout: proposed ASM-ORC-3.]
   273	
   274	1. **Pinned before run.** Every oracle corpus (gold parses, gold record maps, gold
   275	   subgraphs, gold engine results, canonical surface forms) is content-hashed and
   276	   pinned in the family's prereg BEFORE any splice run; growing or editing an oracle
   277	   corpus after first use is a new corpus version and restarts that family's ladder.
   278	2. **Blind authoring.** Gold artifacts are authored blind to any system output —
   279	   from the item + store only. Where the two measured verticals already have gold
   280	   (l3a/a5 gold parses; f2b's d-qa-r record↔item map), those pins are REUSED, not
   281	   re-authored [MEASURED: those corpora exist as frozen pins; reuse is a cost fact].
   282	3. **Gold quality is measured, not assumed.** New gold corpora require dual
   283	   independent authoring on a pre-registered sample (≥10% or 100 items, whichever is
   284	   larger) with agreement ≥ the pre-registered bar (default 0.95 artifact-exactness;
   285	   family preregs may tighten); disagreements adjudicated and logged. A corpus
   286	   failing the bar is not an oracle [STIPULATED: fail-closed gold gate, IG-4 §8].
   287	4. **Answerability pre-tagging.** Every eval item carries an independent tag —
   288	   `answerable-covered | answerable-uncovered | ambiguous | unanswerable` — assigned
   289	   BEFORE any scoring, by the gold-authoring pass. For `ambiguous`/`unanswerable`
   290	   items the gold PARSE artifact is ABSTAIN (abstention is the correct output), and
   291	   for `answerable-uncovered` the gold EXECUTE artifact is the fail-closed refusal —
   292	   so abstentions are creditable and a stage is never penalised for correctly
   293	   refusing [STIPULATED: adopted from PARSE.md §4/§5d (amp-2024, dte-2023,
   294	   triagesql-2020 lineage); this is the abstain-credit rule].
   295	5. **Coverage disclosure.** Items lacking a defined gold artifact at stage j are
   296	   EXCLUDED from stage-j splice cells; the exclusion count and reason are published
   297	   per cell (no silent truncation — the programme's no-silent-caps norm). An
   298	   exclusion rate > 20% in any cell voids that cell's attribution claim
   299	   [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-3].
   300	6. **No oracle leakage into real arms.** The real-regime stages must be bit-isolated
   301	   from oracle corpora at run time (separate process inputs; the harness passes a
   302	   config-scoped artifact bundle) — a real arm that could read gold is a broken run,
   303	   ERR_ORACLE_LEAK, fail-closed [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-3].
   304	7. **Labelling at the artifact level.** Every result row, plot, and report table
   305	   carries `input_regime` (`full-real`, `prefix:<stage>`, `repair:<stage>`,
   306	   `full-oracle`); any figure quoted anywhere outside the report must carry the
   307	   label. Full-real is always the FIRST column [STIPULATED: proposed ASM-ORC-3; the
   308	   anti-smuggling surface].
   309	
   310	---
   311	
   312	## 6. Instrumentation specification
   313	
   314	[STIPULATED throughout: proposed ASM-ORC-4 — implementation choices.]
   315	
   316	- **Home:** `poc/oracle/` (harness code stays out of `encoder/`, repo convention).
   317	  Zero new runtime deps; node/python per existing harness practice; `nice -n 10`,
   318	  checkpointed per box discipline.
   319	- **Stage interface contract:** every participating architecture implements
   320	  `run_stage(stage_id, item_id, upstream_artifact, config) → artifact | ERR_*`, and
   321	  the harness owns the splice: at an oracled stage it substitutes the pinned gold
   322	  artifact and never calls the real stage. Architectures that cannot expose a stage
   323	  boundary cannot claim that boundary's attribution — the limitation is published in
   324	  their prereg (expected for tightly-fused arms at ENCODE, where the probe
   325	  substitutes, §3.1).
   326	- **Config schema** (JSON, hash-pinned per run):
   327	  `{family, vertical, stage_map: [...], regimes: {stage: real|oracle},
   328	  oracle_corpus_hashes: {...}, endpoint_id, seed, n_items, item_corpus_hash}`.
   329	- **Trace record** (JSONL, one per item × config):
   330	  `{item_id, config_hash, tags: {answerability}, perturbation_class,
   331	  stages: [{stage, regime, in_hash, out_hash, outcome: OK|ABSTAIN|ERR_*,
   332	  diverged_from_gold: bool|null, confidence: float|null, cost_us|tokens}],
   333	  endpoint: {answered: bool, correct: bool|null, dangerous_wrong: bool},
   334	  }` — dangerous_wrong = answered ∧ wrong ∧ provenance-attached, the a5-nl S2 event
   335	  definition [MEASURED: a5-nl S2 class, adopted as the event type].
   336	- **Determinism:** deterministic stages byte-reproducible; LM stages seed-pinned
   337	  (greedy or pinned-seed sampling per family prereg); every run twice-executed on a
   338	  1% item sample as a reproducibility smoke check (IG-2).
   339	- **Error codes:** ERR_ORACLE_LEAK, ERR_GOLD_MISSING, ERR_STAGE_CONTRACT,
   340	  ERR_CONFIG_HASH — all fail-closed, run aborts, no partial report (house style: no
   341	  silent fallbacks).
   342	- **Perturbation typing:** the NL item corpus carries a perturbation-class tag per
   343	  phrasing (minimum taxonomy: `label-verbatim | synonym | structural-paraphrase |
   344	  noise(typo/ASR)`; extendable per vertical), enabling the perturbation × stage
   345	  matrix (§7.5) — the Dr.Spider-shaped practice, K>1 phrasings per query from a
   346	  source disjoint from any calibration set [STIPULATED: adopted from PARSE.md §6.5;
   347	  the measured l3a-parse/a5-nl used K=1, below field practice — this protocol
   348	  requires K≥3 phrasings per item for new corpora, K=1 legacy corpora usable only
   349	  for the shakedown].
   350

598	## 3. Architecture design space — named hypotheses
   599	
   600	- DECISION: the design space is enumerated as the named families below — revision 2
   601	  adds **H-PS (NL→program synthesis)** as a first-class family per the review; the
   602	  enumeration is a scoping choice covering the maintainer's directed directions
   603	  plus the seams Programme-2 built, and confers no evidence on any family
   604	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0802 + ASM-0815].
   605	
   606	Evidence-status legend, tied to §0's premises: **supported** (a measured positive
   607	exists at some scope), **cautioned** (a measured negative or unresolved result
   608	bears directly), **unmeasured** (no programme evidence either way). Forward
   609	projections are registered: H-GNN → ASM-0805, H-RULE → ASM-0806, H-DD → ASM-0804 —
   610	all EXTRAPOLATION, never premises. Every family's experiments are structured by
   611	the G1–G5 gate ladder (§4); oracle-input stages are labelled `oracle-diagnostic`
   612	and license no W1 claim [STIPULATED: ASM-0814].
   613	
   614	### 3.1 H-VL — the verifier-loop system (small model + kernel-verify-retry, productised)
   615	
   616	The one audited end-task SIGN, promoted from experiment to *system design*: a
   617	small LM generates; the deterministic engine checks covered claims; failed checks
   618	trigger bounded retry/abstain; the whole loop (with its NL front-end) is the
   619	product being indexed.
   620	
   621	- Rationale: the only family with a measured system-level cost win shape
   622	  (+0.1507 at ~10% cost — formal inputs, alignment-confounded; §0).
   623	- Evidence status: supported at formal-input scope; cautioned by the NL-boundary
   624	  FAILs (the loop's real-input version does not exist until the NLB gate clears)
   625	  and by the unresolved attribution (knull / human f2b-transfer unrun).
   626	- **G1 coverage upper-bound kill (run FIRST, no GPU):** compute
   627	  `Δ_max ≤ Σ_b w_b · κ_b · (1 − a_b^cov)` where w_b is benchmark b's index weight,
   628	  κ_b the store-covered fraction of its items, and a_b^cov the baseline's accuracy
   629	  on covered items — the index gain if EVERY covered item were corrected
   630	  perfectly. If Δ_max < δ_k, the index-mover claim is killed on paper
   631	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0817; the review's formula adopted verbatim]. Given the
   632	  measured external coverage (§0: 0/1,550 define-lane, g8 0/1,000, m0b 0.3542
   633	  friendliest-corpus), this gate is expected to bind hard and early — that is its
   634	  job.
   635	- G4 comparator set (expanded per the review): compute-optimal/adaptive neural
   636	  sampling (not naive best-of-k); a learned neural verifier at matched budget;
   637	  generic structured RAG over the same corpus; grammar/constrained decoding
   638	  without the engine; the same loop with the aligned non-kernel store and with
   639	  the matched generic store.
   640	- Cheapest falsifier: G1 above (~$0); then, if it survives, the R-1 index test on
   641	  natural benchmark text with the NLB-gated front-end inside the measured system.
   642	
   643	### 3.2 H-GNN-{ST, KV, LF} — hybrid GNN over the kernel+world graph
   644	
   645	The kernel + world layer form a typed relational graph (concept URNs, axiom
   646	records, world-layer edges). A small GNN encodes query-relevant subgraphs; the
   647	encoding is fused into the LM at one of three seams:
   648	
   649	| Variant | Fusion seam | Continuity | Expected cost/size delta |
   650	|---|---|---|---|
   651	| H-GNN-ST | GNN output → trained bridge → soft tokens at the input edge | The A2/E5 adapter seam, replicated positive at one rung | +GNN weights (MBs) + adapter; prefill shortens if soft tokens replace text serialisation |
   652	| H-GNN-KV | Graph nodes written as KV pairs into attention (KBLaM-style) | L2b kernel-addressed memory (ladder) | +KV construction cost per query; memory grows with injected node count |
   653	| H-GNN-LF | Late fusion at logits/answer-selection | none (new) | cheapest to build, weakest hypothesised integration |
   654	
   655	- Evidence status: unmeasured as a family; the *seam* is supported (E5 adapter
   656	  PASS at one rung); cautioned hard by nsk1 — text-delivered grounding measured
   657	  net-harmful and internal delivery reached only echo grade with integration
   658	  unresolved (§0), so "the GNN delivers but the LM doesn't use it" is the named,
   659	  measured-adjacent failure mode.
   660	- **Falsifier, rescoped (accepting the review's correction):** "fusion ≤
   661	  text-of-graph on tiny graphs at R-0/R-1" does NOT kill the family — text is
   662	  often superior for tiny graphs; graph encoders may only pay off as graph size
   663	  and reasoning depth grow. The kill is now: fusion ≤ text-of-graph at matched
   664	  information and resource budgets ACROSS a pre-registered sweep of subgraph
   665	  size × reasoning depth (including the D4 held-out relation compositions and
   666	  proof depths) at R-1 — a flat or text-favouring curve over the whole sweep kills
   667	  the family at scope; a depth-increasing fusion advantage promotes it even if
   668	  tiny graphs favour text. Registered projection: ASM-0805.
   669	- Controls (per the review, all pre-registered): same retrieved nodes with no
   670	  edges; shuffled edges and relation labels; MLP/DeepSets/plain-Transformer
   671	  encoders over the same inputs; text serialisation at equal information and
   672	  resource budgets; held-out relation compositions and proof depths; **oracle
   673	  subgraph vs NL-extracted subgraph** — the NL-extracted leg is NLB-gated per
   674	  ASM-0814, the oracle leg is `oracle-diagnostic`.
   675	- **Four-stage decomposition, separately instrumented:** retrieval → graph
   676	  construction → graph encoding → LM use. A positive delivery probe is
   677	  insufficient; the endpoint is causal behavioural improvement on held-out items
   678	  (the nsk1 lesson, enforced by design).
   679	
   680	### 3.3 H-RULE-{CD, KV, AD, ACT, HL} — rules-based inference built into the LLM: the placement analysis
   681	
   682	The maintainer asked *where* deterministic rule-inference should live inside the
   683	model. Five placements, analysed on the axes that matter. Two corrections from
   684	the review are folded into the table and rulings: (a) **constrained decoding is
   685	transport, not inference** — CD enforces an output language; the *engine* must
   686	have already derived the valid continuation set, so H-RULE-CD is precisely
   687	"engine-derived continuation sets delivered via masking", and without the
   688	executor it is just a grammar; (b) **attention weights are not causal
   689	provenance** — KV/HL provenance claims require causal ablation, intervention, or
   690	executor traces [STIPULATED: ASM-0815; corrects technical errors #6 and #8].
   691	
   692	| Placement | Mechanism | Inference cost | Invasiveness | Provenance | Fail-closed | Prior evidence hook |
   693	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
   694	| **H-RULE-CD** constrained-decoding head | engine derives valid continuation sets for covered spans mid-decode; masking delivers them; uncovered decode untouched | engine µs + masking, near-zero overhead | none (no weights touched) | exact (record id per constrained span; the engine's derivation IS the provenance) | yes by construction | L3c (ladder); f2b loop is the unconstrained cousin |
   695	| **H-RULE-KV** KV-side rule memory | rule heads/records inserted as KV pairs keyed by kernel content-hash; attention reads them | KV build per query; grows with fired-rule count | low (no base-weight change) | requires causal ablation/intervention or executor traces — attention maps do NOT qualify | partial | L2b (ladder); KBLaM-family lit |
   696	| **H-RULE-AD** rule adapters | LoRA-class adapters trained to implement rule families; swapped/composed at inference | adapter matmuls, small | medium (trained, but detachable) | none intrinsically | no — must be measured | A2/E5 trained-bridge precedent (input-side only) |
   697	| **H-RULE-ACT** activation-level rule firing | rule conditions detected in activations; firing applies steering vectors / activation edits | probe + edit per layer, small | medium-high (runtime intervention) | weak; causal validation required | no — edits can corrupt off-target behaviour | nsk1 residual channel: delivery ECHO-grade, integration unresolved — the direct caution |
   698	| **H-RULE-HL** dedicated hidden layer | a mid-network layer whose units are pinned to kernel concepts; rule inference as a deterministic map on that bottleneck | in the forward pass (cheap) but always-on | highest (architecture surgery, training from scratch or heavy retrofit) | naming units is NOT interpretability — bottleneck provenance must be causally validated (ablate/patch) | no — bottleneck can throttle general capability | L2a (ladder); the φ-sweep L2c is its dose-response |
   699	
   700	Design ruling from the analysis (a prior ordering, not a verdict):
   701	
   702	- DECISION: Phase-2 tests the placements in the order CD → KV → {AD, ACT} → HL —
   703	  cheapest, least invasive, most provenance-preserving, most fail-closed first;
   704	  HL is deferred until a shallower placement has shown a positive, because every
   705	  deep-internal channel measured so far is unresolved-at-best while the
   706	  external/decode-side seam holds the programme's only positive [STIPULATED:
   707	  ASM-0802; the supporting facts are §0's nsk1 and f2b premises].
   708	- **Endpoint, corrected:** cost parity with the external loop is NOT the sole
   709	  endpoint — an internal placement can be worthwhile via accuracy, coverage, or
   710	  latency-variance improvements even at equal cost. The family head-to-head
   711	  (H-RULE-CD/KV vs H-VL at R-1) is decided on **Pareto frontiers over (accuracy on
   712	  the covered slice, coverage reached, p95 latency, resource vector)**, with the
   713	  pre-registered primary being frontier dominance, not a single-axis comparison
   714	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0815; answers review "Compare Pareto frontiers"]. Registered
   715	  projection: ASM-0806.
   716	
   717	### 3.3a H-PS — NL→program synthesis (new; first-class)
   718	
   719	The review's "promising missing design", adopted as a family rather than a
   720	footnote because it attacks the programme's actual measured wall (the NL
   721	boundary) directly instead of hoping rule knowledge emerges in a hidden layer
   722	[STIPULATED: ASM-0815]:
   723	
   724	- **Design shape:** parse natural input into a small executable DSL (the kernel's
   725	  closed grammar, or a thin typed layer over it); execute deterministically
   726	  (the µs engine); generate the surface answer FROM the checked result; abstain
   727	  with calibration when parse confidence is low (selective prediction — the
   728	  parser emits a calibrated confidence and the system's abstention policy is part
   729	  of the measured product).
   730	- Evidence status: cautioned — this is exactly the l3a-parse/a5-nl path, both
   731	  measured FAIL at scope with the a1-hybrid front-end; H-PS is the *redesign* of
   732	  that path with modern semantic-parsing/program-synthesis methods and calibrated
   733	  abstention, which is why P3-D-PS and P3-D-NLB are both blocked by the
   734	  P3-LR-PARSE lit-review (do not repeat the measured parser failure blindly).
   735	- Relation to P3-D-NLB: NLB is the shared *gate instrument* (retention + S2
   736	  fail-closed measurement per vertical); H-PS is an *architecture family* whose
   737	  front-end, if it clears NLB, becomes the shared front-end other families use.
   738	  They co-design; the gate stays independent of any one family's success.
   739	- Cheapest falsifier: G2/G3 on the two existing verticals — oracle-parse ceiling
   740	  (known: engine exact) vs synthesised-parse retention, against the pinned 0.90
   741	  retention + S2 bars; then the D4 diagnostics with held-out compositions.
   742	
   743	### 3.4 H-RB — retrieve-vs-bake (the store-placement fork, cross-cutting)
   744	
   854	## 4. The gate ladder (information-gaining gates × compute rungs)
   855	
   856	Revision 1 organised progression by compute rung alone, which the review
   857	correctly noted lets an architecture fail ambiguously (graph encoder? retrieval?
   858	NL parse?). Revision 2 organises every family's progression around five
   859	**information-gaining gates**, run cheapest-first, so a failure LOCALISES
   860	[STIPULATED: ASM-0817]:
   861	
   862	| Gate | Question | Input regime | Cost class | What a FAIL localises to |
   863	|---|---|---|---|---|
   864	| **G1 coverage-ceiling** | Can PERFECT oracle use of the store move the index by δ? (`Δ_max ≤ Σ_b w_b κ_b (1−a_b^cov)`) | paper/CPU: census + baseline accuracies | ~$0, no GPU | coverage — kill without touching the mechanism |

  1054	## 7. Honest risks and kill criteria
  1055	
  1056	### 7.1 Programme-level kill — K-P3v2 (quantitative, hardened)
  1057	
  1058	Revision 1's kill contained a subjective escape hatch ("analysts attribute the
  1059	misses to fundamentals") and a soft default (open-ended maintainer fork). Both
  1060	are replaced [STIPULATED: ASM-0818; answers review "The programme-level kill is
  1061	too soft"].
  1062	
  1063	- DECISION: **K-P3v2 (verbatim)** [STIPULATED: ASM-0818]. The competitiveness
  1064	  programme is KILLED — not forked, not re-scoped in place — if ANY of the
  1065	  following pre-registered quantitative conditions holds at its named checkpoint:
  1066	  1. **Coverage ceiling:** after the P3-D-POWER analysis (including any funded
  1067	     coverage-growth ingestion wave completed by the R-1 checkpoint), the
  1068	     perfect-oracle bound Δ_max < δ_1 for EVERY store-dependent family on the
  1069	     frozen R-1 suite — perfect use of the store cannot reach the margin, so no
  1070	     mechanism result can save it.
  1071	  2. **NL boundary:** P3-E-NLB-1 (and its redesigns) fails the 0.90 retention OR
  1072	     the S2 fail-closed safety gate on BOTH verticals after **N = 2 pre-registered
  1073	     redesign cycles** beyond the first attempt.
  1074	  3. **Frontier:** no family's G4 result exceeds the neural/RAG frontier F(B_k)
  1075	     at BOTH R-1 and R-2 (i.e. W1 fails everywhere through R-2 with the frontier
  1076	     comparators properly built).
  1077	  4. **Attribution collapse:** every measured family gain vanishes against the
  1078	     matched generic-store / generic-RAG controls (the win is retrieval + typed
  1079	     storage, available to any baseline, and is absorbed into the frontier).
  1080	  5. **Boundary non-survival:** every family's G2 (oracle-interface) gain fails
  1081	     to survive G3 (natural input) at the pre-registered retention margin, across
  1082	     the N = 2 NLB redesign cycles.
  1083	  6. **Lifecycle domination:** the KOT-LIFE/1 ledger shows S's total cost of
  1084	     ownership dominated by a frontier baseline at ALL THREE pinned amortisation
  1085	     volumes (10^3/10^6/10^9 queries) for every family that passed G4 — a
  1086	     "win" that no deployment volume can ever justify.
  1087	  **Default on kill:** the competitiveness programme TERMINATES; restarting
  1088	  requires a NEW proposal with new evidence or a new mechanism class — there is
  1089	  no open-ended maintainer fork. (The maintainer can of course overrule — but the
  1090	  DESIGN's default is termination, and an overrule is an explicit new decision on
  1091	  the record, not this document's escape hatch.) Surviving single mechanisms
  1092	  (e.g. a G2-positive fusion result) are handed back as SCOPED instruments with
  1093	  their envelopes, not as a continued competitiveness claim [STIPULATED:
  1094	  ASM-0818].

    51	## 1. CORRECTNESS thesis — the verdict
    52	
    53	> **VERDICT (verbatim): INCONCLUSIVE — PENDING (a) an NL-reaching parser [FK-NLB-3] and (b) the semantics core [g2 Π-soundness vs human gold, g3 necessity]. The instrument sub-claim is FEASIBLE-and-DEMONSTRATED but INSTRUMENT-ONLY; every MEASURED crossing from the instrument into real or natural input is, so far, NEGATIVE. SCOPE: the kernel-v0 / molecules-v0 instance, R0–1.7B hosts, the two tested verticals (family/world, code), the one pinned a1-hybrid deterministic front-end, the self-authored covered slice. CONFIDENCE: high on the instrument sub-claim (cross-vendor CONFIRMED); high that the current-build NL boundary and wild-formal boundary are negative at scope; the thesis-level verdict is INCONCLUSIVE because its two decisive legs (semantics core, a better parser) are unrun, not failed.**
    54	
    55	The thesis decomposes into two claims that the evidence separates cleanly, and honesty
    56	requires keeping them apart:
    57	
    58	**Claim C1 — "a training-free deterministic engine that grounds/checks claims EXISTS,
    59	is sound where covered, fails closed off it, and ports across domains" — FEASIBLE,
    60	MEASURED, cross-vendor CONFIRMED, but INSTRUMENT-ONLY.**
    61	
    62	PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a.json + registry/verdicts/a5.json, both audit CONFIRMED; registry/assessments/oracle-coverage.json] The kot-axiom/1 v0 engine is exact and fail-closed on its covered slice at microsecond cost and ports across a domain vertical with a BYTE-IDENTICAL engine binary: l3a (family/world, R0) 600/600 covered-exact (Wilson LB 0.9955) + 300/300 strict refusals (LB 0.9911) + 6/6 planted violations surfaced + 5.29 µs/query; a5 (code, R0) 855/855 covered-exact (LB 0.9968) + 122/122 strict refusals (LB 0.9783) + 3/3 planted violations + 7.82 µs/query, engine byte-identical to the l3a pin and extended only by data + a pure desugaring layer over a no-LLM-extracted code world.
    63	
    64	LOAD-BEARING: This is the strongest, cleanest evidence in the whole registry — and it is evidence about the INSTRUMENT, not about usefulness to any model [MEASURED: both evals are authored against the engines' own stores ("coverage by construction"), so exactness is a consistency-and-licensing property, never world-accuracy; both verdicts are R0 with scale_language_licensed = none; and the a5 map is STRUCTURAL definitions not NSM explications (ASM-0007), l3a directions are stipulated readings (ASM-0004) — the results exercise the FORMALISM, not the NSM SEMANTICS].
    65	
    66	**Claim C2 — "that grounding reaches real/natural input and makes a model measurably more
    67	correct" — INCONCLUSIVE, and every MEASURED boundary crossing is NEGATIVE so far.**
    68	
    69	The measured boundary results, at full prominence and each scoped to its own envelope:
    70	
    71	- PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a-parse.json, verdict FAIL] NL-boundary, family/world vertical: the a1-hybrid deterministic front-end retains only 251/527 = 47.6% of gold-parse covered exactness (Wilson UB 0.5121 vs the 0.90 bar) — a decisive FAIL. The mechanism is a STRUCTURAL paraphrase ceiling: the label-verbatim stratum parses 76.4% but the PARAPHRASE stratum parses 0/261 = 0.0% (single-label mapper, no synonym table). The failure is SAFE here — the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL did NOT fire (8/527 wrong, LB 0.0086 < 0.02); mis-parses become refusals.
    72	- PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/a5-nl.json, verdict FAIL, two independent causes] NL-boundary, code vertical: 356/855 = 41.6% (UB 0.4443) — FAIL — AND the S2 dangerous-wrong KILL FIRED (43/855 = 5.0% wrong, LB 0.0394 ≥ 0.02). This is the DANGEROUS failure mode the design named worse than any refusal: the frame-layer ROLE_DIR direction table flips containment orientation (contained-in 24, where-defined 18) into a real-but-wrong answer *with provenance attached*.
